title: @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload and Resource Budgets · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs @aws-sdk/lib-storage upload and resource budgets from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for lib-storage multipart adoption contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin @aws-sdk/lib-storage 3.1087.0 with its peer S3 client line and wrap Upload behind characterized queueSize, partSize, leavePartsOnError, progress, abort, cleanup, and result contracts.
Inventory v2 ManagedUpload and hand-written multipart behavior, map queue, part, progress, abort, cleanup, and result differences, then migrate to v3 Upload behind contract tests.
Choose PutObjectCommand or lib-storage Upload from body size and replayability, and validate Node Readable, Web stream, Blob, progress-event, checksum, and completion ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8931 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload workers, part buffers, abort ownership, and cleanup policy; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin @aws-sdk/lib-storage 3.1087.0 with its peer S3 client line and wrap Upload behind characterized queueSize, partSize, leavePartsOnError, progress, abort, cleanup, and result contracts.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
